2605.02782.title: When Audio-Language Models Fail to Leverage Multimodal Context for Dysarthric Speech Recognition
2605.02782.paper_summary: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems remain brittle on dysarthric and other atypical speech. Recent audio-language models raise the possibility of improving performance by conditioning on additional clinical context at inference time, but it is unclear whether these models can make use of such information. We introduce a benchmark built on the Speech Accessibility Project (SAP) dataset that tests whether diagnosis labels, clinician-derived speech ratings, and progressively richer clinical descriptions improve transcription accuracy for dysarthric speech. Across matched comparisons on nine models, we find that current models do not meaningfully use this context: diagnosis-informed and clinically detailed prompts yield negligible improvements and often degrade word error rate. We complement the prompting analysis with context-dependent fine-tuning, showing that LoRA adaptation with a mixture of clinical prompt formats achieves a WER of 0.066, a 52% relative reduction over the frozen baseline, while preserving performance when context is unavailable. Subgroup analyses reveal significant gains for Down syndrome and mild-severity speakers. These results clarify where current models fall short and provide a testbed for measuring progress toward more inclusive ASR.
2605.02782.paper_summary_zh: 自動語音識別（ASR）系統在口齒不清和其他非典型語音上仍然脆弱。最近的音頻語言模型提高了在推理時根據額外臨床背景來改善性能的可能性，但尚不清楚這些模型是否能利用這種信息。我們介紹了一個基於語音無障礙項目（SAP）數據集的基準，測試診斷標籤、臨床醫生衍生的語音評級以及逐步豐富的臨床描述是否能提高口齒不清語音的轉錄準確性。在九個模型的匹配比較中，我們發現當前模型並未有效利用這些背景信息：基於診斷的和臨床詳細的提示僅帶來微不足道的改善，並且往往會增加詞錯誤率。我們用依賴上下文的微調來補充提示分析，顯示使用多種臨床提示格式的LoRA適應達到了0.066的詞錯誤率，相對於凍結基準減少了52%，同時在缺乏上下文的情況下保持性能。子群分析顯示，唐氏綜合症和輕度重度說話者的表現有顯著提升。這些結果澄清了當前模型的不足之處，並提供了一個測量向更具包容性的ASR邁進的試驗平台。
2605.02782.author: Pehuén Moure et.al.
2605.02782.authors: Pehuén Moure, Niclas Pokel, Bilal Bounajma, Yingqiang Gao, Roman Boehringer, Longbiao Cheng, Shih-Chii Liu
